onChain.signal: Exchange netflow is zero (partial Dune data, cold/custody only — unreliable). LTH accumulating +2,354 BTC/day (+0.056% liquid) is mildly positive. MVRV estimated ~1.5 (neutral zone). Net: flat. Score capped at 0 due to data gap.

etfInstitutional.signal: ETF net outflow -$290M (-3,725 BTC, -0.089% liquid) on 2026-05-15. Well below $264M/day baseline inflow — this is a net negative divergence of ~$554M vs baseline. Strong institutional de-risking signal. Full -2.

derivatives.signal: Funding rate -0.0002% per 8h (-0.2% annualized) is slightly negative — longs are being paid, indicating mild bearish positioning/over-hedging. Fear & Greed 31 (Fear) is correlated; scored on shared axis. Contrarian: slight bullish lean from over-hedged shorts. Net +1.

cmeBasis.signal: CME futures basis unavailable. Cannot assess institutional forward premium. Neutral by default.

macro.signal: DXY 78.9 is constructive for BTC (weak dollar, bullish). VIX 18.4 is benign/normal. 10Y yield 4.59% is elevated and a headwind for risk assets. Mixed signals net to zero.

sentiment.signal: Fear & Greed already scored under derivatives (shared axis per methodology). Not double-counted here. Options skew 9.7 (put IV 46% vs call IV 37%) confirms elevated fear — consistent with derivatives reading.

stablecoin.signal: USDT+USDC at $266.7B, 7d delta -$0.9B. Regime: STABLE per DefiLlama, but marginal contraction in dry powder. Per scoring rules: CONTRACTING = -1.

scoreDecomposition.scaleNote: Range -10 to +10. Composite: 0 + (-2) + 1 + 0 + 0 + 0 + (-1) = -2. Rounded to -1 given LTH accumulation and negative funding as partial offsets.

brief.biasReason: ETF outflows dominate; price 4.6% below 200d SMA with stablecoin contraction.
brief.headline: BTC Slides 3.2%; ETF Outflows and Put Skew Signal Caution

correlationRegime.implication: Without live BTC-QQQ correlation data, assume moderate regime typical of fear-driven sell-offs; macro risk-off likely driving BTC lower in sympathy with equities.

priceAnalysis.trend: BTC at $77,958 is below both the 200d SMA ($81,752) and 20d SMA ($79,301), but above 50d SMA ($75,192). Price is in a bearish structure on the daily — below all key medium-term averages. Phase A accumulation zone remains valid ($50,673–$81,752).
priceAnalysis.keyLevel: $81,752 (200d SMA / Phase A→B threshold) is critical resistance. $75,192 (50d SMA) is next support. $79,301 (20d SMA) is immediate overhead resistance.
priceAnalysis.realizedPriceContext: Realized price heuristic floor ~$50,673 (HEURISTIC_FLOOR derivation). Current price ~54% above realized price floor — not distressed, but 88% through Phase A range with price retreating from the boundary. STH cost basis ~$75K — spot price remains above, limiting forced selling.

whaleSignal.netflowBTC: 0 BTC (Dune partial data — cold/custody addresses only, not representative of full exchange balance delta)
whaleSignal.historicalContext: Zero netflow from partial Dune dataset is uninformative. During prior Phase A accumulation periods, meaningful whale accumulation registers -2,000 to -8,000 BTC/day on full exchange tracking.
whaleSignal.detail: Dune data covers cold/custody wallets only — exchange hot wallet delta unavailable. Cannot confirm accumulation or distribution from on-chain source alone. LTH net position +2,354 BTC/day provides partial positive signal.
whaleSignal.actionable: Do not interpret zero netflow as neutral — data is incomplete. Rely on Binance taker pressure and ETF flows as primary directional signals today.

binancePressure.confluence: Net taker sell pressure (-1,006 BTC, 42.2% buy ratio) confirms bearish directional bias and aligns with ETF outflows — no divergence between Binance order flow and institutional positioning.

fundingRates.signal: Mildly negative funding — short sellers are paying longs at a de minimis rate. Historically, deeply negative funding (-0.01%+ per 8h) marks capitulation lows. Current level is not extreme — merely reflecting slight bearish lean in perpetual markets.
fundingRates.detail: Annualized -0.2% is negligible. Not a contrarian squeeze signal yet. OI at 101,126 BTC ($7.9B) is moderate — no excessive leverage buildup. Slight positive: market is not overloaded with longs that could cascade.

openInterest.regime: 101,126 BTC / $7.9B — moderate absolute level. At $77,958 spot, OI-to-price ratio is in mid-range. No crowded positioning.
openInterest.detail: Stable OI alongside a -3.2% price decline and mild negative funding suggests organic spot selling rather than leveraged liquidation cascade. Bears are not over-positioned.

mvrvSignal.implication: MVRV ~1.5 sits in the neutral-to-fair-value zone (below 2.0 = not overextended, above 1.0 = above cost basis). Neither screaming buy nor sell.
mvrvSignal.cycleContext: In prior cycles, MVRV 1.2–1.8 during Phase A represented accumulation opportunity for 12–18 month holders. Consistent with mandate horizon.

stablecoinSignal.detail: USDT+USDC combined $266.7B with 7d delta of -$0.9B. Marginal contraction. While the regime is labeled STABLE by DefiLlama, any negative delta reduces dry powder available for deployment into spot BTC.
stablecoinSignal.signal: Mild bearish — stablecoin supply not growing to support potential BTC bid. Contracting liquidity at the margin.

macroContext.dxySignal: BULLISH for BTC — DXY at multi-year lows historically correlates with BTC strength. Structural USD weakness remains a medium-term tailwind.
macroContext.realYield: 10Y nominal 4.59% — real yield elevated given current inflation estimates. Headwind for non-yielding assets including BTC.
macroContext.realYieldSignal: BEARISH — elevated real yields compress multiples on risk assets. Partially offset by weak DXY.
macroContext.gold: $4,527.8 (-0.62% 24h). BTC/Gold ratio 17.22. Gold at historically elevated levels — BTC underperforming gold in current regime. Gold outperformance signals safe-haven demand over risk-on crypto.
macroContext.fedWatch: No live Fed data in today's feed. 10Y at 4.59% suggests market pricing minimal near-term cuts. Higher-for-longer environment mildly negative for BTC.
macroContext.detail: Net macro picture: DXY weakness is constructive but insufficient to offset real yield headwind and risk-off gold preference. VIX 18.4 is not alarming — systemic stress is absent. Mixed-to-mildly-bearish macro overlay.

todayAction.size: Deploy 20–25% of remaining DCA cash reserve into IBIT/FBTC at current levels ($77,958). Phase A mandate supports accumulation. Do not deploy full reserve — preserve dry powder for potential retest of $75,192 (50d SMA).
todayAction.trigger: Confirmed daily close above $79,301 (20d SMA) with ETF flows returning to positive territory (>$0) would justify adding a second tranche. Alternatively, any intraday flush to $75,000–$75,500 (50d SMA zone) is a high-conviction add point.
todayAction.dynamicStop: $49,153 hard stop (mandate floor). Tactical alert level: daily close below $73,000 (well below 50d SMA) would warrant pausing DCA and reassessing. Phase A remains valid as long as price holds above $50,673.
todayAction.scoreJustification: Composite score -1 (CAUTION), but mandate-aligned: Phase A at 88% progression, MVRV ~1.5, LTH accumulating, negative funding (not overextended longs), and 1-2 year horizon absorbs near-term ETF outflow headwinds. ACCUMULATE is correct — but size conservatively given deteriorating ETF trend.

cmeBasis.signal: Cannot assess institutional forward premium without live CME basis data. Treat as neutral input.
cmeBasis.detail: CME basis data unavailable today. In prior sessions with similar spot weakness, basis has compressed toward flat or mild backwardation — which would signal institutional hedging demand. Monitor for next available print.

catalystWatch.event: US Digital Asset Market Structure / Clarity Act Progress
catalystWatch.note: Training knowledge: Clarity Act defines BTC as commodity under CFTC jurisdiction. Senate floor vote expected mid-2026. Passage would unlock institutional participation and ETF product expansion. Regulatory news unavailable today — timing is estimate from training knowledge.

10Y yield at 4.59% suggests market pricing no imminent cut. A dovish pivot or pause signal would be strongly bullish for BTC; hawkish surprise could accelerate current correction. Training knowledge estimate — no live Fed data in today's feed.

May 15 single-day outflow of -$290M bears watching. If outflows persist >3 consecutive days and exceed -$500M cumulative, tactical pause on DCA warranted. Reversal to inflows above $500M/day would trigger Phase B add protocol per mandate.

Supply issuance now ~450 BTC/day post-halving. LTH accumulation at +2,354 BTC/day (5.2x daily issuance) indicates structural demand absorption. This dynamic strengthens over Q3–Q4 2026 as miner selling pressure normalizes.

brief.analystNote: BTC is consolidating at $77,958 — 88% through Phase A accumulation range and 4.6% below the critical 200d SMA / Phase A→B trigger at $81,752. Today's session is characterized by a convergence of bearish near-term signals: -$290M ETF outflows (well below baseline), net taker sell pressure on Binance (42.2% buy ratio), elevated put skew (9.7), and marginal stablecoin contraction — all of which warrant caution on short-term positioning. However, the structural picture remains intact for a 1-2 year mandate holder: MVRV estimated ~1.5 is not historically stretched, LTH net accumulation at +2,354 BTC/day (5.2x post-halving daily issuance) confirms patient capital is absorbing supply, negative funding rate eliminates the long squeeze risk present at cycle peaks, and DXY at 78.9 provides a meaningful macro tailwind. Phase anchor derivation is flagged as HEURISTIC_FLOOR (realized price unavailable from Dune and CoinMetrics), meaning the $50,673 floor and $81,752 Phase B trigger are heuristic estimates — the true realized price could differ by ±5–10%, which investors should factor into stop-loss precision. The recommended action remains conservative ACCUMULATE (20–25% of DCA reserve) with a tactical add trigger at the 50d SMA ($75,192) and a Phase B confirmation signal set at a daily close above $81,752 with ETF inflows exceeding $500M.
brief.riskWarning: A sustained ETF outflow streak exceeding 5 consecutive days with cumulative net >-$1.5B would signal institutional capitulation, potentially breaking $75,192 support and invalidating the near-term accumulation thesis.
